entries.garrytan/gbrain: This project functions as a self-wiring knowledge graph that automatically extracts entities and establishes typed links between data points. It utilizes a hybrid search architecture that boosts ranking via backlinks and includes 26 modular skills for comprehensive data ingestion and task management.
entries.vectorize-io/hindsight: Hindsight implements a biomimetic memory system designed to help agents learn through dedicated retain, recall, and reflect workflows. It simplifies long-term memory management via a two-line LLM wrapper that organizes facts into evolving mental models.
entries.greyhaven-ai/autocontext: This recursive context harness uses a multi-agent loop to extract and persist validated knowledge across complex scenarios like negotiation and tool workflows. It is unique for its ability to distill stable agent behaviors into local models using MLX integration.
entries.stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm: Hermes-LCM provides a lossless context management engine that uses a hierarchical Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) to compact messages without losing information. It relies on an immutable-first SQLite store to provide agents with structured tools for historical retrieval.
entries.elkimek/honcho-self-hosted: This repository enables private, cross-session persistence by providing a self-hosted backend for the Honcho memory framework. It is designed for easy deployment via Docker and supports any OpenAI-compatible provider or local inference server.
entries.yoloshii/ClawMem: ClawMem is a local-first memory vault that uses a hybrid retrieval strategy combining vector search, BM25, and graph traversal. It focuses on on-device privacy, using local models to automate decision capture and generate handoffs between agent sessions.
entries.plur-ai/plur: Plur introduces a shared memory layer that stores persistent data as open-format YAML engrams to ensure portability across multi-agent systems. It features zero-cost hybrid retrieval and a biological-inspired mechanism where memories strengthen or decay based on usage frequency.
entries.amanning3390/flowstate-qmd: Developed for the Hermes 2026 Hackathon, this tool features an anticipatory cache that prefetches relevant context into a local 'intuition file.' It integrates as an MCP server, utilizing SQLite-vec and node-llama-cpp to provide privacy-focused RAG for coding workflows.

entries.mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills: This comprehensive library provides 754 structured cybersecurity skills mapped to major frameworks like MITRE ATT&CK and NIST. It equips Hermes with specialized security domains, ensuring the agent can operate within standardized defensive and offensive workflows.
entries.conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing: This tool audits content for 36 distinct AI writing patterns and applies a multi-tier word replacement strategy to eliminate linguistic tells. It is essential for Hermes users who need to produce natural, human-like prose that avoids automated detection.
entries.wondelai/skills: A modular, cross-platform library that brings architecture, UX design, and marketing frameworks to agentskills.io-compatible agents. It allows Hermes to quickly expand its capabilities through a dedicated CLI and marketplace system.
entries.DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills: This project implements the Agent Skills open specification for Pydantic AI with a focus on token efficiency. By using progressive disclosure of skill metadata, it allows Hermes to manage complex Python-based skills without exhausting context windows.
entries.Agents365-ai/drawio-skill: This skill enables Hermes to generate complex diagrams, such as UML and system architectures, directly from natural language descriptions. It includes an automated self-check loop that allows the agent to independently identify and fix layout issues in the resulting XML.
entries.smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills: Designed for blockchain interaction, this skill set provides standardized workflows for the Chainlink Runtime Environment and CCIP. It allows Hermes to perform cross-chain sends, monitor contracts, and discover decentralized oracle network resources.
entries.tlehman/litprog-skill: This literate programming tool allows Hermes to weave runnable code and prose documentation into unified markdown files. It features a 'tangler' to extract source files and a synchronization mechanism to ensure edits stay consistent across both formats.
entries.esaradev/icarus-plugin: Icarus provides Hermes with shared memory across instances using markdown-based fabric directories. It focuses on 'training your replacement' by extracting decision-quality data and tagging it for fine-tuning future model iterations.
entries.chigwell/skilldock.io: This registry offers an OpenAPI-driven SDK for discovering and managing reusable AI skills with recursive dependency resolution. It uniquely supports skill monetization through integrated payment features for both TON and Stripe.
entries.black-forest-labs/skills: The official skill set for FLUX image generation, providing Hermes with precise prompting guidelines and API integration patterns. It supports advanced features like JSON structured prompting and exact hex color specification for high-fidelity visual tasks.
entries.Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory: This meta-skill plugin monitors active workflows to automatically detect and propose new, reusable skills. It streamlines development by generating both SKILL.md documentation and Python scaffolds directly from the agent's successful patterns.
entries.ReinaMacCredy/maestro: Maestro is a harness for long-running tasks that maintains shared state on disk and facilitates structured context transfers between agents. It provides a read-only TUI and JSON snapshots, allowing Hermes to track complex missions across multiple sessions.
entries.tiann/execplan-skill: This skill enables autonomous execution of multi-step tasks with built-in checkpoints and recovery procedures. It ensures Hermes can handle long-running operations safely by providing real-time progress tracking and rollback capabilities.
entries.cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill: This progressive MCP client uses a three-layer lazy loading pattern to minimize token usage when defining tools. It maintains persistent connections to multiple servers via a socket-based daemon, allowing Hermes to scale its toolset efficiently.
entries.armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge: This forge converts existing repositories and developer documentation into verified agentskills.io-compliant skills using AST analysis. It ensures high accuracy by linking every AI instruction back to specific source file line numbers.
entries.amanning3390/hermeshub: HermesHub serves as a community browsing and installation hub featuring automated security scanning for prompt injections. It supports one-click installation via the Hermes CLI and includes a marketplace for creators using micropayment protocols.
entries.PederHP/skillsdotnet: This project brings the agentskills.io specification to the C# and .NET ecosystem, including MCP resource templates. It provides vendor-specific shortcuts that allow Hermes to integrate seamlessly with .NET-based development environments and tools.

entries.numtide/llm-agents.nix: This project provides a comprehensive suite of Nix packages for terminal-based AI coding agents, including Hermes. It automates daily updates for all included tools and supports a mix of open-source and proprietary binary-based development agents.
entries.TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw: Hermesclaw focuses on security by providing hardware-level enforcement of network, filesystem, and process boundaries for the agent. It features three security policy presets and includes secure gateways for integrating Hermes with Telegram, Discord, and Slack.
entries.0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent: This repository offers a NixOS module for declarative management of Hermes configurations, documents, and systemd services. It prioritizes security through sops-nix integration and supports a hybrid skill model that combines Nix-managed assets with interactive CLI installations.
entries.rookiemann/portable-hermes-agent: Designed for Windows users, this no-install desktop application provides a GUI and over 100 tools without requiring admin rights or Docker. It features integrated GPU monitoring, local model management, and a workflow engine for scheduled automation.
entries.xmbshwll/hermes-agent-docker: This project provides a streamlined Docker sandbox that includes both the official Hermes installation and mini-swe-agent. It supports persistent state through mounted directories and allows users to build specific Hermes versions using build arguments.
entries.JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server: Targeting production environments, this deployment configures the Hermes Gateway as a reboot-safe systemd service for Ubuntu and Debian. It is optimized for headless operation and enables the autonomous execution of recurring AI cron jobs.
entries.ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes: This Docker Compose stack integrates with Portainer to deploy Hermes alongside a ttyd web terminal interface. It simplifies the deployment lifecycle by automating image builds through GitHub Actions and the GitHub Container Registry.

entries.builderz-labs/mission-control: This self-hosted orchestration platform manages multi-agent workflows using a lightweight SQLite backend with zero external dependencies. It is ideal for Hermes users who require real-time task monitoring and strict quality control through the built-in Aegis review system.
entries.swarmclawai/swarmclaw: Swarmclaw provides a self-hosted runtime for building autonomous agent swarms that support over 20 providers, including local Ollama and Hermes. It distinguishes itself by enabling agents to learn new skills from conversations and maintain persistent memory across delegated tasks.
entries.howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness: This framework focuses on meta-optimization by using an automated outer-loop search to refine the harness code used for Hermes Agent evaluations. It orchestrates complex benchmarks like TBLite and TB2, tracking performance frontiers through structured candidate mutation.
entries.runtimenoteslabs/gladiator: Gladiator creates a competitive arena where two autonomous AI companies, powered by Hermes Agent, compete for GitHub stars. The system orchestrates nine specialized agents and visualizes their real-time execution, code generation, and scoreboard metrics on a live dashboard.
entries.1ilkhamov/opencode-hermes-multiagent: This project coordinates a massive pipeline of 17 specialized agents dedicated to tasks ranging from infrastructure to security. It enforces rigorous quality gates by routing all outputs through mandatory reviewer and tester agent loops to ensure production-ready code.
entries.supermodeltools/bigiron: Bigiron implements an AI-native software development lifecycle by providing Hermes with structural code graphs via the Supermodel MCP. It automates an eight-phase development process, using graph-diff gates to ensure architectural integrity at every step from planning to health checks.
entries.Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill: This framework utilizes ACP-style delegation to route tasks between Hermes subagents and external models like Codex or Claude Code. It prioritizes operational safety by isolating delegation workflows to prevent context pollution and enforcing strict timeouts and output limits.

entries.junhoyeo/tokscale: This Rust-based TUI tracks token consumption and real-time costs across over 20 AI coding agents, including Hermes and Claude Code. It leverages LiteLLM pricing data to provide developers with detailed usage analytics and 3D contribution visualizations.
entries.joeynyc/hermes-skins: A visual customization toolkit that allows users to personalize the Hermes terminal interface with custom ASCII banners, spinners, and color schemes. Themes are managed via YAML configuration files that support inheritance, making it easy to create and share unique community skins.
entries.AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC: This project ports the Hermes Agent brain and its 46 core skills directly into the Claude Code Channel for native execution without external processes. It specifically enhances the environment with MLOps capabilities for vLLM inference, GGUF management, and reinforcement learning training.
entries.unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser: An AI-native browser designed for autonomous agent control that exposes its state and navigation through Model Context Protocol (MCP) endpoints. It provides a human-visible interface for auditing agent workflows while maintaining persistent sessions and named checkpoints.
entries.Abruptive/Ankh.md: A framework for deploying scoped Hermes Agents that are restricted to specific folders with local configurations and project-specific memories. It allows developers to seamlessly switch between global and local agents, each utilizing their own unique toolsets and instructions.
entries.roli-lpci/lintlang: This static analysis tool evaluates agent configurations and system prompts for structural failure modes without incurring LLM costs or latency. It integrates into CI pipelines to gate deployments based on HERM v1.1 scoring and automated quality verdicts.
entries.0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes: A dedicated migration utility that automates the transition of personas, memories, and skills from OpenClaw to the Hermes Agent ecosystem. It handles the complex mapping of model configurations and credentials into the Hermes YAML format to ensure workspace continuity.
entries.42-evey/evey-setup: An automated deployment script that stands up a full Hermes Agent stack using Docker, LiteLLM, and Qdrant in minutes. It comes pre-configured with 29 community plugins and is optimized for routing to free models or local GPU inference.

entries.nesquena/hermes-webui: This zero-build web interface provides a three-panel layout that mirrors the full Hermes Agent CLI experience for both desktop and mobile browsers. It allows users to manage sessions, chat with the agent, and browse workspace files through a lightweight Python and vanilla JavaScript architecture.
entries.outsourc-e/hermes-workspace: A native web workspace that unifies chat, terminal access, and a memory browser into a single interface for managing agent skills. It features real-time SSE streaming to visually render agent tool calls and supports diverse local backends like Ollama and vLLM.
entries.xaspx/hermes-control-interface: This self-hosted dashboard acts as a multi-agent gateway for configuring and monitoring multiple agent profiles simultaneously. It stands out for its detailed token analytics, which track costs by model and platform, alongside integrated cron management and system metrics.
entries.dodo-reach/hermes-desktop: A native macOS workspace that utilizes direct SSH connections to interact with the agent without requiring local file mirroring or daemons. It provides a profile-aware environment featuring a multi-tabbed terminal with customizable themes and live color controls.
entries.fathah/hermes-desktop: Designed as a companion app, this tool simplifies the initial setup of Hermes Agent through guided installation and dependency resolution. It bridges the agent with external messaging platforms like Discord and Telegram while supporting a wide array of LLM providers.
entries.sanchomuzax/hermes-webui: This monitoring-focused dashboard specializes in process oversight, offering real-time tracking of gateway status and session costs. It includes a SQLite-powered full-text search for inspecting conversation histories and a read-only interface for managing scheduled cron jobs.
entries.Euraika-Labs/pan-ui: A comprehensive AI workspace that features an integrated marketplace for discovering and installing new skills and MCP servers. It streamlines deployment with an automated setup wizard that supports daemon and systemd execution for persistent agent profiles.
entries.pyrate-llama/hermes-ui: Featuring a glassmorphic design, this single-file React interface requires no npm dependencies for deployment. It includes a dedicated live artifact panel for rendering HTML and SVG code blocks alongside a real-time system performance monitoring dashboard.
